title: Serve the AMS marketing homepage from the same Worker that serves the protocol, not from a separate site
content: The AMS Worker now answers GET / and GET /index.html with a self-contained HTML document. Rationale: same-origin colocation lets the homepage drive the real Day 1 endpoints (POST /v1/accounts, POST /v1/{ns}/conversations) without proxying, without CORS theatre on the protocol surface, and without an extra deploy pipeline. It also keeps the entire public face of AMS inside one Worker version — the homepage and the protocol move together, which is the right invariant for a project whose pitch is "AMS is the wire." This is reversible (a separate static site could supersede this later) but the cost of reversal is low: pull homepage.ts and the two route additions out of index.ts. Encoded as additive marketing surface in worker/src/homepage.ts (~76KB of inline HTML+CSS+JS) plus six lines of routing in worker/src/index.ts. SPEC.md, wrangler.toml, accounts.ts, auth.ts, conversations.ts, types.ts, util.ts not touched. Day 1 PoC contract preserved end-to-end (POST /v1/accounts → 201 verified, POST /v1/{ns}/conversations → 201 verified after the change).

title: Open CORS only on /healthz; /v1/* stays same-origin only
content: Added Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * (plus matching allow-methods and allow-headers and a preflight handler for OPTIONS) to /healthz. /v1/* routes are deliberately not opened. Rationale: /healthz is a read-only liveness check that is safe to expose to any origin (it returns {ok, host, ts} and nothing else), and opening it lets third parties build status dashboards, lets the homepage poll both ams.klappy.dev and ams.truthkit.ai from a single page, and gives oddkit-style telemetry probes a clean target. /v1/* stays same-origin only because (a) the homepage shares an origin with the protocol so it does not need cross-origin access, (b) credential issuance and conversation creation are write operations that should not invite drive-by browser invocation from arbitrary tabs, and (c) the protocol's own clients are agents and CLI tools that do not care about CORS. This is the minimum viable opening — the smallest surface that makes the homepage's wire-status pills real.

title: The homepage MUST NOT misrepresent what AMS does today
content: The homepage shows four genuinely-live data sources and labels everything else as a simulation. Live: (1) /healthz polling against both production hosts every 8s with green/red wire-status pills, (2) real POST /v1/accounts followed by real POST /v1/{ns}/conversations through the same-origin fetch path with the live response shown verbatim, (3) live oddkit telemetry pulled directly from oddkit.klappy.dev/mcp via JSON-RPC for the dogfood-credibility section, (4) real curl examples in the protocol section that copy-paste against the deployed Worker. Clearly labelled as in-browser simulation: the token-stream theatre demo, which dramatises the SPEC §3.2 hackathon scene with two agents writing into their own streams and the wire delivering each stream's tokens to the other side. The simulation runs entirely in the browser because the WebSocket data plane is Day 2 scope and does not exist yet. The page tells the visitor exactly that: the theatre section header carries an SIM badge and the roadmap section names the demo as Day 2 work.

title: The oddkit MCP is publicly fetchable from any browser with full CORS — making "live telemetry" sections genuinely cheap to build
content: Verified during the build that POST https://oddkit.klappy.dev/mcp accepts unauthenticated JSON-RPC calls from a browser fetch with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * and returns SQL telemetry results synchronously. This means any marketing surface, status page, or public dashboard can show real oddkit usage numbers (top tools, payload shapes, consumer leaderboards) by issuing a single fetch on page load — no API key, no proxy, no scraping. The AMS homepage uses this to render an animated bar chart of oddkit's last-7-day tool leaderboard, which double-functions as proof that the same governance/observability primitives AMS will eventually expose are already running in production for a sibling project. Rule of thumb to remember: when telemetry is part of the dogfood story, the marketing site should consume it the same way an external integrator would. If the integrator path requires a proxy, build the proxy first; if it does not, just call the MCP directly from the browser.

title: tsc clean and end-to-end smoke verified locally — homepage and Day 1 contract both intact
content: Ran npx tsc --noEmit in worker/ → exit 0 with no diagnostics. Started wrangler dev locally on 127.0.0.1:8799 with simulated KV and the .dev.vars peppers. Verified: GET / returns 200 text/html 74487 bytes with the correct title, all eight expected sections present (TOKEN STREAM ROUTING, WIRE STATUS, TWO DOORS, THEATRE, PROTOCOL, TELEMETRY, ROADMAP, CREED) and 20+ DOM IDs for the interactive controls. GET /index.html returns the same 74487 bytes (alias works). GET /healthz returns 200 with {ok, host, ts} and now carries Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *, Vary: Origin, allow-methods: GET, HEAD, OPTIONS. OPTIONS /healthz returns 204 with full CORS preflight headers. Day 1 contract preserved: POST /v1/accounts returns 201 with a valid acc_/ams_sk_ pair, and POST /v1/{ns}/conversations with that credential returns 201 with conversation_id, alias, magic_link, stream_id, stream_name. Unknown routes still 404. Inline JS payload 22.6KB, inline CSS 29.4KB, single <script> and single <style> block, structurally well-formed.

title: Live deploy is gated on three operator-side steps that have been pending since Day 1 closeout — homepage will go live the moment those land
content: The homepage is fully built and locally verified, but ams.klappy.dev currently does not resolve in DNS and ams.truthkit.ai returns 503 because the Worker is not yet deployed. Per the Day 1 closeout journal (2026-05-02-day1-worker-control-plane.tsv), three operator-side steps gate the first real deploy: (1) DNS records for ams.klappy.dev pointing to the Worker, (2) Workers Build wired to the repo so the deploy hook in package.json runs on push, (3) AMS_CREDENTIAL_PEPPER and AMS_PERMISSIVE_TOKEN_PEPPER bound as secrets via the Cloudflare dashboard. None of those are blocked on code; all three are operator-console actions. When they land, the next git push triggers the deploy and the homepage appears at both hosts simultaneously alongside the protocol surface. No code-side handoff is required — the homepage is in the same Worker bundle.

title: Day 2 (DOs + WebSocket data plane) can retrofit the theatre demo with the real backend in one wiring change
content: The homepage's token-stream theatre section is built as an in-browser simulation that replicates SPEC §3.2 verbatim: two agents join a conversation, each writes into its own stream, the wire delivers each stream's tokens to the other side, with self-echo structurally excluded per D0009. When Day 2 ships the Conversation DO with WebSocket fan-out, the same UI can be repointed at the real backend by swapping the simulation's local emit/receive functions for new WebSocket('/v1/{ns}/conversations/{alias}?t={token}') calls — the rendering, log views, agent A/B layout, and self-echo discipline are all already in place. The simulation is intentionally written so that this swap is mechanical: emit() and onToken() are the only functions that need to change, and their signatures already match the wire frame shape from SPEC.
